description:
Locations: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:200, 224, 255 — three function signatures all take `user_pin: &Option<Zeroizing<String>>`.

What the code does: passes a borrowed Option containing a Zeroizing<String>. The function only reads the PIN if it is Some.

What idiomatic Rust would do: take `Option<&Zeroizing<String>>` (or even `Option<&str>`). Why it matters:
  - `Option<&T>` is a smaller, more flexible type — callers can pass `option.as_ref()` from any source, not just a borrow of the exact same Option.
  - It composes with combinators: `pin.map(|p| ...)` reads top-down rather than `if let Some(pin) = user_pin { ... }`.
  - clippy::ref_option flags this in pedantic mode.

The change at the call site is `&self.user_pin` -> `self.user_pin.as_ref()` and `user_pin` -> `user_pin.as_ref()` inside spawn_blocking closures. The fn body changes are minimal: `if let Some(pin) = user_pin` works unchanged for `Option<&T>`.

This is not a hot path — PIN handling happens on session open. The win is purely readability and aligning with clippy::pedantic.
title: Three fns take &Option<Zeroizing<String>>; should be Option<&Zeroizing<String>>
